records.ety: [LL. collateralis; col- + lateralis lateral. See Lateral.]

defs: Coming from, being on, or directed toward, the side; as, collateral pressure.

quotes: If by direct or by collateral hand They find us touched, we will our kingdom give . . . To you in satisfaction.

defs: Related to, but not strictly a part of, the main thing or matter under consideration; hence, subordinate; not chief or principal; as, collateral interest; collateral issues.
quotes: That he [Attebury] was altogether in the wrong on the main question, and on all the collateral questions springing out of it, . . . is true.

defs: Tending toward the same conclusion or result as something else; additional; as, collateral evidence.
quotes: Yet the attempt may give Collateral interest to this homely tale.

defs: Descending from the same stock or ancestor, but not in the same line or branch or one from the other; -- opposed to lineal.
notes: ☞ Lineal descendants proceed one from another in a direct line; collateral relations spring from a common ancestor, but from different branches of that common stirps or stock. Thus the children of brothers are collateral relations, having different fathers, but a common grandfather.

defs: Collateral security; that which is pledged or deposited as collateral security.
